title: Quiz: How well do you know Saudi Labor Law?
description: Test your knowledge of the Saudi Labor Law (issued by Royal Decree No. M/51, as amended up to Royal Decree No. M/44 dated 8/2/1446H)

questions.question: What is the official weekly rest day for all employees under the Labor Law?
options: Fully up to the employer to decide
questions.hint: Think of the day an employer may substitute for some employees with the labor office's approval, while still allowing them to observe their religious duties.
questions.reference: Article (104) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: What is the maximum number of actual working hours per day if the employer applies the daily standard?
questions.hint: This number drops to just 6 hours during Ramadan for Muslim employees.
questions.reference: Article (98) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: How many days is the basic annual leave for an employee with less than 5 years of service?
questions.hint: This number rises to a full 30 days once the employee completes 5 consecutive years with the same employer.
questions.reference: Article (109) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: What is the maximum total duration of a probation period in an employment contract, no matter how many agreements are made?
questions.hint: This period is roughly equivalent to six months, and any leave days the employee takes during it are not counted within it.
questions.reference: Article (53) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: For an indefinite-term contract with a monthly wage, how many days' notice must the employer give the employee if the employer wants to end the contract?
questions.hint: The notice period the employer must give differs from — and is longer than — the one the employee must give if they are the one ending the contract.
questions.reference: Article (75) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: How is the end-of-service gratuity calculated for each of the first five years of an employee's service?
options: A third of a month's wage
questions.hint: This rate doubles to a full month's wage for each year of service beyond the first five years.
questions.reference: Article (84) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: An employee's resignation request is automatically deemed accepted if a certain period passes without a response from the employer. What is that period?
questions.hint: The employer has the right to defer acceptance of the resignation for longer than this if work interests require it, provided a justified written explanation is given.
questions.reference: Article (79 bis) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: An employer may dismiss an employee without gratuity, notice, or compensation if the employee is absent without a legitimate excuse for a consecutive period exceeding how many days (after a prior written warning)?
questions.hint: The same article contains another case involving intermittent absence during a single contract year, with a threshold roughly double this number.
questions.reference: Article (80), Paragraph (7) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: What is the maximum percentage an employer may deduct monthly from an employee's wage to recover a loan given to the employee (without the employee's written consent to a higher rate)?
questions.hint: This percentage differs from other deduction cases, such as disciplinary fines or enforcing court judgments, which can reach up to three-quarters of the wage.
questions.reference: Article (92) of the Saudi Labor Law

questions.question: An employee is entitled to a paid leave to perform Hajj once during their employment, provided they have completed a continuous period of service with the employer of at least:
questions.hint: The leave itself ranges between 10 and 15 days, inclusive of the Eid al-Adha holiday.
questions.reference: Article (114) of the Saudi Labor Law
